task.title: Slack DM-send silent failure — missing_scope diagnosis & fix

events.content: Not a cloud code or adapter-version bug — rule out before blaming code

raw.question: Why does a /slack/users/<U>/messages writeback push up but never send a DM, with no error and no dead-letter?
raw.chosen: Treat the cloud send-path as present+deployed and look elsewhere
alternatives: Missing cf processor routing for users path (ruled out: SLACK_UPSERT_WRITEBACK_PATH includes users/.../messages, provider-executor.ts:1203-1205, deployed 2026-05-12)
Adapter version not deployed (ruled out: adapter-slack 0.3.9 already resolves DM->post_dm; 0.3.9->0.3.10 changed only READ-side path-mapper; cloud#1997 fixed DM read, not send)
cf/bridge missing DM dispatch (ruled out: cf dispatchSlackDirectMessage + bridge fallback both present pre-#1997)
raw.reasoning: Every cloud-side send component (executor allowlist, cf provider, bridge fallback, adapter resolution) was present and deployed. The gap had to be runtime config/scope, not code.

events.content: ROOT CAUSE: Slack app missing im:write scope for conversations.open

raw.question: What is the actual failing leg for DM send when channel writebacks work?
raw.chosen: Missing im:write bot scope on conversations.open
alternatives: user_not_found / cannot_dm_bot (excluded by the persisted op error)
Nango proxy mapping (excluded: channels dispatch fine through the same proxy)
raw.reasoning: DM send calls conversations.open BEFORE chat.postMessage; conversations.open to a user requires the im:write bot scope, while channel chat.postMessage only needs chat:write. Missing im:write -> Slack returns {ok:false,error:missing_scope} (HTTP 200); missing_scope is NOT in the retryable set (providers/slack.ts:476-485) -> permanent_failure -> acknowledgePermanentFailure: no retry, no dead-letter, no surfaced error -> silent no-DM. CONFIRMED via relayfile getOp on op_27419: status=failed, lastError=missing_scope, attemptCount=0.

events.content: Fix applies in THREE places, in order — reconnect alone is insufficient

raw.question: How does the operator apply the scope so the new token carries it?
raw.chosen: Slack app scope+reinstall -> Nango dashboard requested scopes -> re-OAuth
alternatives: Just reconnect Slack (insufficient: the OAuth requested-scope set lives in the Nango dashboard slack-relay, not the repo; reconnect without adding it there re-grants the old set)
raw.reasoning: The OAuth authorize URL requests scopes from the Nango dashboard config (not auto-derived from the Slack app, not in the repo). So add im:write(+mpim:write) in BOTH the Slack app (manifest + reinstall) AND the Nango slack-relay dashboard, THEN re-OAuth; existing tokens get no retroactive grant.

events.content: Diagnostic mechanic: read the op lastError, not the writeback-consumer logs

raw.question: Where does the Slack error actually surface?
raw.chosen: op lastError (relayfile getOp / writeback list) + cf audit event in cloud-web-worker
alternatives: wrangler tail relayfile-writeback-consumer (dead end: the batch executor queue-consumer.ts:71 logs ONLY writeback.start, never the slackError)
raw.reasoning: The dispatch result is acked to the op (status+lastError) and forwarded to the external audit sink (cloud-web-worker '[relayfile] writeback audit event'). getOp requires the X-Correlation-Id header (400 without).

retrospective.summary: Outbound Slack DM-send failed silently (lastError=missing_scope) because the Slack app lacked im:write for conversations.open. Not a cloud code/adapter bug. Fixed by adding im:write/mpim:write in BOTH the Slack app AND the Nango dashboard + re-OAuth. Verified: re-test op_27514 status=succeeded, bidirectional DM working. Repo sync drafted as cloud#2014.
retrospective.approach: Rule out deployed code first (executor allowlist, adapter version, cf/bridge dispatch), then read the persisted op lastError via relayfile getOp — not the writeback-consumer logs — to localize the fault to runtime scope.
